theorem putnam_1980_a5
(P : Polynomial ℝ)
(xeqs : ℝ → Prop)
(Pnonconst : P.degree > 0)
(hxeqs : ∀ x : ℝ, xeqs x = (0 = (∫ t in (0)..x, P.eval t * Real.sin t) ∧ 0 = (∫ t in (0)..x, P.eval t * Real.cos t)))
: {x : ℝ | xeqs x}.Finite :=
sorry
problem.informal_statement: Let $P(t)$ be a nonconstant polynomial with real coefficients. Prove that the system of simultaneous equations $0=\int_0^xP(t)\sin t\,dt=\int_0^xP(t)\cos t\,dt$ has only finitely many real solutions $x$.
problem.coq_statement:
Section putnam_1980_a5.
Require Import Reals Coquelicot.Coquelicot.
Theorem putnam_1980_a5
    (n : nat)
    (npos : gt n 0)
    (coeff : nat -> R)
    (hcoeff : coeff n <> 0)
    (p : R -> R := fun x => sum_n (fun i => coeff i * x ^ i) (S n))
    (h1 : nat -> Prop := fun a => RInt (fun x => p x * sin x) 0 (INR a) = 0)
    (h2 : nat -> Prop := fun a => RInt (fun x => p x * cos x) 0 (INR a) = 0)
    : exists (m: nat), forall (b: nat), h1 b /\ h2 b -> lt b m.
Proof. Admitted.
End putnam_1980_a5.
problem.isabelle_statement:
theory putnam_1980_a5 imports
Complex_Main
"HOL-Computational_Algebra.Polynomial"
"HOL-Analysis.Interval_Integral"
begin

theorem putnam_1980_a5:
  fixes P :: "real poly"
  and xeqs :: "real \<Rightarrow> bool"
  defines "xeqs \<equiv> \<lambda> x :: real. 0 = interval_lebesgue_integral lebesgue 0 x (\<lambda> t. poly P t * sin t) \<and> 0 = interval_lebesgue_integral lebesgue 0 x (\<lambda> t. poly P t * cos t)"
  assumes Pnonconst: "degree P > 0"
  shows "finite {x :: real. xeqs x}"
  sorry

end
